title: Intensely Savory Ukrainian Myeolchi Anchovies Tagine
description: A macrobiotic-friendly, lazy Sunday Ukrainian dish featuring myeolchi anchovies cooked in a tagine, resulting in a funky, glassy-jelly texture. This recipe combines the savory flavors of anchovies with the heartiness of Ukrainian cuisine.
instructions: Heat the olive oil in the bottom of a clay or ceramic tagine over medium heat.
Add the chopped onion and cook until it is translucent and starting to caramelize.
Add the minced garlic and cook for an additional 1-2 minutes, until fragrant.
Add the chopped carrots and potatoes, and cook for 5 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Add the myeolchi anchovies, tagine spice blend, and sea salt, and stir to combine.
Add 2 cups of water to the tagine, and bring the mixture to a boil.
Reduce the heat to low, cover the tagine, and simmer for 20 minutes, or until the vegetables are tender.
Remove the tagine from the heat, and let it cool slightly before serving.
